source_document: Arkansas HB1877 (Act 977, As Engrossed) - Amendment of Arkansas CSAM Laws
annotation_provenance: EA1-2/EA1-1 bill-level gold seed (2026-07-13). References the committed engrossed source via bill_text_file so the bill-level agent sees the exact full text the pipeline feeds it (margin numbers, engrossment headers, mid-page breaks). Expected enforcement fields hand-checked against the enacted text. Conservative: only the criminal-penalty classification stated unambiguously IN THIS BILL is asserted. Adds a second bill-level law AND a second enforcement SHAPE (criminal, vs AZ SB1359's per-day civil penalty) so the bill-level baseline isn't monoculture. This is an amendment-markup (engrossed) bill per EA1-1 stratification.

annotation_notes.criminal_penalties: SECTION 8 amends § 5-27-603 and states 'Possession or use of child sexual abuse material Electronic facilitation of child sexual abuse is a Class B felony.' — a criminal penalty, stated verbatim.
annotation_notes.private_right_of_action: This is a criminal statute amending offenses in the Arkansas Criminal Code; it creates no private cause of action, so the correct value is false, not null.
annotation_notes.max_civil_penalty_usd_intentionally_omit: No civil penalty is stated — the bill sets a felony CLASS, not a dollar amount. Correct enforcement_agent behavior is max_civil_penalty_usd = null (the felony class must NOT be coerced into a dollar figure). Omitted here because null-valued expected fields are not scored; a future negative-assertion mode could pin 'must be null'.
annotation_notes.enforcing_body_intentionally_omitted: No enforcing agency is named — criminal enforcement runs through ordinary prosecutorial channels the bill does not enumerate. Asserting a specific body would be a hallucination target; left unscored pending EA1-1 annotation.
annotation_notes.penalty_tiers_note: The bill classifies one offense (Section 8) as a Class B felony; the other amended sections (2, 3, 6, 7) redefine conduct/carve-outs without stating their own new penalty amounts, so there is no numeric penalty-tier structure to encode here.
